user_message: Can you work with me on migration architecture for Consolidated Messenger?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 14:26 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 16:14 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 12:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger migration architecture

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Consolidated Messenger: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-E trap (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-E failure mode (MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-E -- MSX proposals duplicate already-tracked items
